ip_outcome_note: Card name is Cara Liss; PokemonTCG.io confirms card is a Trainer Supporter named 'Cara Liss'; oracle text explicitly references 'Rare Fossil cards' matching her in-game function at Route 6 Galar. Bulbapedia confirms she is a named NPC in Pokemon Sword & Shield who operates a fossil revival machine at Route 6. Vision description (white lab coat, rocky cave setting) matches published character design. Internal Pokemon IP, no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Cara Liss is a named NPC in Pokemon Sword & Shield, located on Route 6 in Galar where she operates a revival machine that assembles fossil Pokemon from mixed fossil parts. Unlike previous fossil revival NPCs, Cara Liss's machine combines non-matching head and body fossils, producing chimeric fossil Pokemon: Arctozolt, Dracovish, Dracozolt, or Arctovish. [Bulbapedia: Cara Liss, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Bulbapedia: Cara Liss, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Cara Liss has a verbal tic of ending sentences with 'huh' in the games — a character-writing quirk that contrasts with her scientific role. Her appearance per Bulbapedia: short black hair in a bob, tan skin, black eyes with white pupils, muddy face and clothes from fieldwork. Illustrated on this card by Hitoshi Ariga, a veteran Pokemon manga artist best known for the Pokemon Adventures manga adaptation. [Bulbapedia: Cara Liss, via WebSearch snippet; PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-149 metadata]
sources: Bulbapedia: Cara Liss, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Oracle text: 'Search your deck for up to 2 Rare Fossil cards and put them onto your Bench.' The 'Rare Fossil' item card functions as a placeholder on the Bench that can evolve into one of the Galar fossil Pokemon (Dracozolt, Arctozolt, Dracovish, Arctovish). Cara Liss's card role — pulling two fossils from the deck — directly mirrors her in-game mechanic of requiring two fossil pieces per revival. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-149 oracle text]

related_cards.name: Rare Fossil
related_cards.note: Item card required by Cara Liss's oracle text — the bench placeholder for fossil Pokemon

related_cards.name: Dracovish V
related_cards.note: One of the four chimeric fossil Pokemon Cara Liss revives in-game

related_cards.name: Arctozolt
related_cards.note: Fossil Pokemon in the same set — direct in-game product of Cara Liss's machine
